title: Community clinic intake page
problemStatement: Patients may abandon intake if the page implies care is unavailable, expensive, or dependent on documentation they do not have.
audience: New patients booking free or low-cost care from a mobile phone
userGoal: Understand whether the clinic can help today and what information is needed to request care.
businessGoal: Route patients to the right intake path while reducing incomplete requests.

contextDetails.label: Decision needed
contextDetails.value: - [ ] Agree which reassurance copy must appear before the form.

contextDetails.label: Out of scope
contextDetails.value: Clinical triage logic, insurance verification, and translated final copy.

contextDetails.label: Contributors
contextDetails.value: Clinic ops, patient advocate, content design, engineering

items.title: Reassurance before action
items.content: The highest-priority content that makes the appointment request feel possible.
annotations: Keep reassurance and the appointment request together before preparation details.

children.title: Care is available regardless of insurance status
children.content: The clinic supports uninsured patients, Medicaid patients, and people unsure about eligibility.
annotations: Show before forms so people do not abandon due to cost anxiety.
Plain-language note should be translated in later content pass.

children.title: Request an appointment
children.content: Ask for name, preferred contact method, reason for visit, urgency, and best times to be contacted.
annotations: Urgent symptoms route to a call-now message.
Contact preference controls whether phone or SMS consent copy appears.

items.title: What to bring
items.content: List ID if available, current medications, recent paperwork, and questions for the care team.
annotations: Content should not imply documents are required for care.
